title: The Download: introducing the Nature issue
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Introducing: the Nature issue When we talk about “nature,” we usually mean something untouched by humans. But little of that world exists today.

title: Beehiiv rolls out new creator tools, including webinars and customizable paywalls
description: Beehiiv is clearly done being just a newsletter platform based on today's launch of a new webinar feature, customizable paywalls, and more.

title: Will fusion power get cheap? Don’t count on it.
description: Fusion power could provide a steady, zero-emissions source of electricity in the future—if companies can get plants built and running. But a new study suggests that even if that future arrives, it might not come cheap. Technologies tend to get less expensive over time. Lithium-ion batteries are now

title: India’s app market is booming — but global platforms are capturing most of the gains
description: Non-gaming apps, led by streaming and AI, are driving growth, even as India's spending per user lags global peers.

title: Tesla just increased its spending plan to $25B — here’s where the money is going
description: Tesla's planned capex for 2026 is three times higher than what the company has historically spent. Its CFO said, as a result, Tesla will have a negative free cash flow the rest of the year.

title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
description: Google has introduced a host of new automated functions into Workspace, all of which are driven by Workspace Intelligence, its new AI system.

title: Hands on with X’s new AI-powered custom feeds
description: X's AI-powered custom timelines are replacing Communities, with Grok-curated feeds...and new ad slots.

title: Sam Altman’s Orb Company Promoted a Bruno Mars Partnership That Doesn't Exist
description: “To be clear, we were never approached … nor were we in any discussions regarding a partnership or tour access,” a spokesperson for the artist tells WIRED.

title: Crypto scam lures ships into Strait of Hormuz, falsely promising safe passage
description: Ship attacked by Iran after possibly falling for safe passage crypto scam.

title: Tesla reports Q1 2026 earnings: Still profitable
description: Car sales are up, battery sales and emissions credits are down.

title: Our newsroom AI policy
description: How Ars Technica uses, and doesn't use, generative AI.

title: Lawsuit: Nintendo is getting tariff refunds—its customers should get them instead
description: Lawsuit demands Nintendo pass Trump tariff refunds on to its customers.

title: RFK Jr. won't back CDC director on vaccines as agency scraps positive data
description: Kennedy's tesimony sets up another clash over vaccines with next CDC director.

title: 5 AI Models Tried to Scam Me. Some of Them Were Scary Good
description: The cyber capabilities of AI models have experts rattled. AI’s social skills may be just as dangerous.

title: AI Tools Are Helping Mediocre North Korean Hackers Steal Millions
description: One group of hackers used AI for everything from vibe coding their malware to creating fake company websites—and stole as much as $12 million in three months.

title: The Download: introducing the 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: AI needs a strong data fabric to deliver business value
description: Artificial intelligence is moving quickly in the enterprise, from experimentation to everyday use. Organizations are deploying copilots, agents, and predictive systems across finance, supply chains, human resources, and customer operations. title: 3 things Michelle Kim is into right now
description: Isegye Idol If you thought K-pop was weird, virtual idols—humans who perform as anime-style digital characters via motion capture—will blow your mind. title: Join Our Livestream: Musk v. Altman and the Future of OpenAI
description: On May 8, we’re going live to answer your questions about the Musk v. Altman trial that could determine the fate of OpenAI.

title: The Pope’s Warnings About AI Were AI-Generated, a Detection Tool Claims
description: Pangram Labs’ updated Chrome extension puts warning labels on AI slop as you scroll your social feeds.

title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
